title: Corsair (Derriphan-class)
content:

ship/Legends|Capital ship]] |commission= |destroyed=[[3996 BBY]], [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]] |retired= |battles=*[[Battle at Primus Goluud]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] *[[First battle of Korriban]] *[[Second battle of Korriban]] *[[Battle at the Denarii Nova]] *[[Battle of Kemplex Nine]] |affiliation=*[[Sith Empire/Legends|Old Sith Empire]] *[[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Exar Kun's Sith Empire]] |navy=*[[Sith invasion fleet]] |fleet= |owners=*[[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] *[[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] |captains=*Naga Sadow *Exar Kun *[[Aleema Keto]] |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|Here is the power to wrench fire from the stars…|Exar Kun|}} The '''''Corsair''''' was a [[Derriphan-class battleship|''Derriphan''-class battleship]] that played a major role in two [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] wars a millennium apart. A [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], the ''Corsair'' contained [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-enhancing [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] of immense power that were able to tear out the core of a [[Star/Legends|star]]. The ''Corsair'' was initially one of the [[Flagship/Legends|flagships]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] [[Sith invasion fleet]] during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. After the war's tide turned against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], Sadow used the ''Corsair'' to flee [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]], using the power of the ship to cause solar flare activity in the star [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]] as cover for the retreat to the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. There, the ''Corsair'' saw heavy action in [[First battle of Korriban|two]] [[Second battle of Korriban|consecutive]] battles, the first against a group of Sith rivals and the second against pursuing Republic forces. To avoid defeat in the second battle, Sadow used the remaining ships in his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] as a barricade and fled the scene in the ''Corsair''. Flying through the twin stars of the [[Denarii Nova]], Sadow used the ship's power to [[Battle at the Denarii Nova|destroy]] any pursuers. He then settled with his [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] crew on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], building the [[Temple of Fire]] around the ''Corsair'', where it remained entombed for a millennium. In [[3997 BBY]], the ''Corsair'' was discovered by [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], a newly ascended Dark Lord of the Sith. With the ''Corsair'' under his control, Kun struck up an alliance with [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] to wage [[Great Sith War/Legends|war]] against the Republic and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. The ''Corsair'' was the vessel that flew to the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and retrieved Qel-Droma after his arrest and [[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma|trial]] for a [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|failed attack]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. After the treachery of Qel-Droma's lover, [[Aleema Keto]], was discovered following his rescue, Kun and Qel-Droma formulated a plan to rid themselves of both her and the bumbling [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Crado]]. Command of the ''Corsair'' was given to Keto and Crado, and they were directed to lead an [[Battle of Kemplex Nine|attack]] on the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Kemplex Nine]] to lure Republic forces there. After successfully drawing Republic attention, Keto used the ''Corsair''{{'s}} power to rip the core out of a star in the [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]] and use it as a weapon, obliterating their enemies. However, she found herself unable to control the ship's power; a chain reaction caused a [[Cron Supernova/Legends|supernova]] in the cluster, which destroyed the ''Corsair'', all according to Kun and Qel-Droma's plan. ==Description== {{Quote|I can sense the Sith power vibrating through the walls.|Aleema Keto|}} The ''Corsair'', a [[Derriphan-class battleship|''Derriphan''-class battleship]], was a well-[[Armor/Legends|armored]] [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] that could sustain a large amount of damage despite its lack of [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]]. Its heavy armor allowed the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to concentrate its [[Laser/Legends|fire]] on individual enemy ships, although its small complement of six [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] forced it to rely on its point-defense weapons for most of its anti-fighter protection. 215 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long, the ''Corsair'' required a crew of only twenty-five but could hold up to 850 passengers, 5,000 tons of cargo, and one [[Standard year/Legends|year's]] worth of consumables. Able to contain two [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] alongside six starfighters, the [[Battleship/Legends|battleship]] was armed with a battery each of [[Point-defense laser cannon/Legends|point-defense laser cannons]] and heavy [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launchers, which each had a payload of sixty [[Missile/Legends|missiles]]. The ship had several antenna-like protrusions extending from its roof, and two blocks of six [[Engine/Legends|engines]] each on its stern. The ''Corsair'' put into use a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]], and had a Class 4 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. The ''Corsair'' had a sort of cone shape, with the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] located at the top and several jagged spires at the bow. There were similar appendages located at the bottom of the ship. Surrounded by [[Viewport/Legends|viewports]], the command deck was where many of the ship's crewmembers worked, monitoring the ship's systems and weapons. The bridge also contained at least one [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]]. The ship had light-green plating. The ''Corsair'' was rather nimble for its size, even able to navigate between [[Star/Legends|stars]] in tightly packed [[Star cluster/Legends|clusters]]. These characteristics all were standard for the ''Derriphan''-class [[Warship/Legends|battleship]]. However, the ''Corsair'' was no ordinary battleship. Within the ''Corsair''{{'s}} command deck, a platform with many [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-enhancing [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] rose from the ship's floor. These crystals had immense, nigh-unimaginable power; they were able to interfere with stars themselves, the scope of their abilities ranging from creating solar flare activity to tearing out the star's core. However, the incredible power of these crystals was very hard to control by individuals not trained in their use. ==History== ===War and abandonment=== {{Quote|The powers of the Sith give me more weapons than the Republic could ever imagine!''"
"''Naga Sadow—how could you do this? We'll ''all'' be destroyed!''"
"''Yes, Gav—you know ''exactly'' what I have done.|Naga Sadow and Gav Daragon, as Sadow unleashes the power of the ''Corsair'' during the Battle at Primus Goluud|}} [[File:CorsairLightning.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''Corsair'' triggers solar flares during the Battle at Primus Goluud.]] The ''Corsair'' was one of the battleships that served [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] during his rule over the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] in a secluded [[Sith Worlds/Legends|region]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], around [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. However, the ''Corsair'' was different from the other ''Derriphan''-class warships employed by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] during that [[Time/Legends|time]], as it was equipped with powerful Sith magic crystals that were potent enough to wrench out the heart of a star. That year, due to the arrival of two [[Navigator/Legends|explorers]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], [[Human/Legends|Human]] siblings [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], the expansionist Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] learned the location of Republic [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and began preparations to [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|invade]]. The ''Corsair'' was selected as one of the main [[Flagship/Legends|flagships]] of Sadow's [[Sith invasion fleet|invasion fleet]]. After amassing a sizable force, the Sith [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] jumped through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and began their attack on the Republic. But toward the end of the war, Gav Daragon, who by that time had become one of Sadow's highest-ranking [[General/Legends|generals]], started to doubt if he was doing the right thing by serving the Sith. After an encounter with his sister during a [[Battle of Koros Major (Great Hyperspace War)|battle]] at their [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]], Gav decided to rebel against the Dark Lord. Daragon confronted Sadow at the star [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]], where Sadow was sequestered while he [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] the war effort, and fired upon [[Naga Sadow's Mothership|his]] [[Sith Meditation Sphere]]. The ever-devious Sadow tricked Daragon into meeting him face-to-face on the Sith craft, but by the time Daragon reached the Meditation Sphere, Sadow had escaped. A Republic fleet soon arrived on the scene; when Gav offered to help them battle the Dark Lord, Sadow recalled what was left of his forces and returned to Primus Goluud in the ''Corsair''. A brief [[Battle at Primus Goluud|skirmish]] ensued between the two sides, a battle that ended when Sadow used the power of the ''Corsair'' to trigger solar flares in Primus Goluud, [[Death/Legends|killing]] Gav Daragon and covering the Sith retreat back to their worlds. After fleeing the Republic fleet at Primus Goluud, the ''Corsair'' and the rest of Sadow's battered invasion fleet returned to Sith Space. However, they were promptly greeted by an old nemesis, [[Ludo Kressh]], a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and rival of Naga Sadow's who was believed dead. As it happened, Kressh had faked his death and built a massive fleet, claiming the title of Dark Lord of the Sith and awaiting Sadow's return from the war. After some verbal sparring between Sadow and Kressh, a [[First battle of Korriban|battle]] broke out. Sadow, commanding his forces from the ''Corsair'', eventually won the battle after ordering a damaged vessel to crash into Kressh's flagship, killing the self-proclaimed Dark Lord. Unfortunately for him, Sadow's forces received very little time to recover, as shortly after they were [[Second battle of Korriban|attacked]] by a Republic fleet led by [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]], who had followed [[Galactic coordinates/Legends|coordinates]] given to them by Gav Daragon to reach the Sith worlds. The ''Corsair'' was damaged heavily during this second battle, with its systems failing and weapons spent. The battle was lost, but Sadow had one more trick up his sleeve. Ordering his remaining ships to form a barricade, Sadow maneuvered the ''Corsair'' in between the [[Binary star/Legends|twin stars]] of the [[Denarii Nova]] and [[Battle at the Denarii Nova|used]] the ship's power to create massive solar flare activity and destroy any pursuing ships. Teta's forces departed, thinking he was dead, but Sadow had escaped in the ''Corsair''. Now the only remaining ship from the invasion fleet, the ''Corsair'' jumped into hyperspace and limped to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], an isolated [[Moon/Legends|moon]] in the Outer Rim. Sadow landed the ''Corsair'' in a burned clearing on the [[Tree/Legends|forested]] moon and began life anew with his [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] followers. The exiled Dark Lord built the [[Temple of Fire]] around the ''Corsair'', where it would remain for over a thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]].[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 5]] ===Rediscovery=== {{Quote|Of course! The ''spacecraft'' that brought the original Sith renegades to the jungle moon of Yavin! If it still operates, I won't have to be stranded on this moon!|Exar Kun discovers the ''Corsair'' on Yavin 4|}} [[File:Exar_Kun_Corsair.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Exar Kun orders Massassi to recover the ''Corsair''.]] In [[3997 BBY]], the ''Corsair'' was rediscovered by a young Human and former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]. Kun had been sent to Yavin 4 by the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of former Dark Lord [[Freedon Nadd]], who sought to turn the powerful [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-user]] to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. However, Kun used his newfound dark [[Force power/Legends|powers]] to dispel Nadd's spirit, claiming the title of Dark Lord for himself. Curious about the secrets of the dark side, Kun began research into Yavin 4's mysteries, leading him to discover the ''Corsair'' in the [[Sith temple/Legends|Temple]] of Fire in which it had resided for a thousand years. It was a fortunate turn of events for Exar Kun, as his personal starship, the ''[[Starstorm One]]'', had been destroyed by hostile Massassi upon his arrival on Yavin 4, leaving him stranded.[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] Even after a thousand years of disuse, the ''Corsair'' was still perfectly preserved—although it needed maintenance before it was fully spaceworthy again—and Kun soon set out on a mission aboard the mighty warship. Before Kun destroyed the spirit of Freedon Nadd, he felt Nadd contact other students to warn them of Kun's power; therefore, Kun resolved that he would find these other students of Nadd and kill them. Kun flew the ''Corsair'' to the planet Empress Teta, where Human [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], another trainee in the dark ways, was staying. However, Kun did not kill Qel-Droma; instead, on orders from the spirit of dead Dark Lord of the Sith [[Marka Ragnos]], he took the Jedi as his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] and pledged to wage war on the Republic and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]] [[File:ThirstyAleema.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Onboard the ''Corsair'', Aleema Keto learns from Exar Kun how to create supernovae.]] By the [[3996 BBY|next year]], the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] between Kun's forces and the Republic was in full swing. However, after a [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|failed assault]] on the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Ulic Qel-Droma was taken [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]] and put on [[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma|trial]] in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] [[Senate Hall|chamber]]. Upon learning of Qel-Droma's detainment, [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|leader]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans that had pledged allegiance to the Sith cause, flew to Yavin 4 to inform Kun of the development. The two discussed the recent matter beside the ''Corsair'', and they agreed that they needed to fly to Coruscant and free Qel-Droma. They did so in the ''Corsair'', along with a large Massassi force, and [[Duel in the Senate|succeeded]] in their mission.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] With Qel-Droma safely delivered from Republic custody, Mandalore the Indomitable revealed that Qel-Droma's lover, the Human [[Aleema Keto]], a skilled Sith [[Magic/Legends|magician]] and ruler of the Empress Teta [[Star system/Legends|system]], had betrayed him and allowed him to be captured. After the group's return from Coruscant, Kun and Qel-Droma devised a plan to rid themselves of Keto and [[Crado]], a [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who had failed the Sith Lords too many times. Assigning their targets to lead an [[Battle of Kemplex Nine|attack]] on the [[Space station/Legends|outpost]] of [[Kemplex Nine]], Kun and Qel-Droma gave Keto and Crado the ''Corsair'' to command, as Kun taught Keto how to use the ship's dark power. The ''Corsair'' traveled to Kemplex Nine and assaulted the station, luring a Republic fleet and a team of Jedi to investigate. Meanwhile, Keto and Crado hid the ''Corsair'' in the heart of the [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]], a dense group of stars located close to Kemplex Nine. When the Jedi arrived, Aleema Keto used the ship's ancient power to tear out the core of one of the Cron Cluster's stars and use it as a weapon, utterly destroying the joint Jedi/Republic force. However, it was then that Keto and Crado realized that they had been tricked. Just as Kun and Qel-Droma wanted, the star imploded, even after Keto shut down the weapon's power source. The incident created a massive [[Cron Supernova/Legends|supernova]], which obliterated the ancient ''Corsair'' and killed both Keto and Crado. Around the era of the [[Treaty of Coruscant]], [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Gnost-Dural]] mentioned the ''Corsair'' during one of his [[Galactic Timeline (in-universe)|video logs]] about the history of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], recorded in the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]].{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 10: The Exar Kun War|int=Timeline 10: The Exar Kun War}} ==Commanders and crew== {{Quote|I have very little left of my glory—a single ship, a few followers, and a Massassi crew.|Naga Sadow|}} The original [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''Corsair'' was the Dark Lord of the Sith Naga Sadow, who used the vehicle as one of the flagships of his invasion fleet during the Great Hyperspace War. Sadow used it as his personal battleship, and, after seeing action in several major battles under Sadow's command, it ended up being the sole remaining ship from the Sith fleet. One thousand years later, the ''Corsair'' became the possession of Exar Kun, who used the ship in his own war on the Republic. The ''Corsair'' was also commanded on its final mission by Aleema Keto and Crado, who were on board when the ship was destroyed by the Cron Supernova. The ''Corsair'' required a crew of twenty-five individuals; during both the Great Hyperspace War and the Great Sith War, the crew was usually comprised of Massassi. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Corsair'' first appeared in a flashback in the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] miniseries. The issue was written by [[Tom Veitch]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]], illustrated by [[Chris Gossett]], and released by [[Dark Horse Comics]][[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] on [[October 4]], [[1994]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54571/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_dark_lords_of_the_sith_1994_1|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Dark Lords of the Sith (1994) #1}} ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] *[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] {{1st}} {{Flash}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 5]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 5]] {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Fallen Empire]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *{{FactFile|90|NAD 1-4|The Shadow of Freedon Nadd}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 10: The Exar Kun War|int=Timeline 10: The Exar Kun War}} {{Hologram}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Derriphan-class battleships]] [[Category:Flagships]] [[Category:Sith superweapons]] [[Category:Starship superweapons]] [[Category:Starships of Exar Kun's Sith Empire]] [[Category:Starships of the Sith Empire]]
